page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 5669 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core tests, and Passmark CPU scores. The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, which contribute to strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong read and write performance, although the overall Passmark memory score remains in the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of the D96lds_v6 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive in-memory databases but highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. The combination of 96 dedicated vCPUs, high-speed local storage, and strong multi-core throughput makes this server well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, cryptography, and medium-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 5669 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance excels in multi-core execution, achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks. The processor supports Intel AMX and AVX-512, driving strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing. With a low memory-to-CPU ratio, it represents a cost-efficient choice for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is highly suited for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local storage, and advanced matrix extension support for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and AI inference workloads
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and web serving
Optimized for compute-bound tasks, cryptography, and parallel processing
